post.description: Compute CGST, SGST, IGST from taxable amount and rate.
Compute 234B and 234C interest for advance tax defaults.
Check ITC eligibility against GSTR-2B data.
Compute net GST payable after eligible ITC deduction.
Apply statutory component-wise ITC utilization and return cash payable.
Compute TDS amount based on section and PAN availability.
Resume a failed read job from its saved cursor; financial writes are never retried here.

get.description: Return source-level GST evidence, including ITC decisions still needing a CA.

post.description: Record the accountant's explicit ITC decision; this is not a provider-side write.

get.description:
GET /api/connectors/zoho/gst-summary?client_id=&period=

Fetches output tax from Zoho Books (sales invoices, period-filtered)
and 2B-matched ITC (input tax) from the reconciliation engine.

Period format: MMYYYY (e.g. '062026' for June 2026).

GET /api/connectors/zoho/gstr3b-json?client_id=&period=&gstin=

Is intentionally blocked until the GSTR-3B working has real tax components.

GET /api/connectors/zoho/gstr3b-tables?client_id=&period=

Returns only a working draft when jurisdictional tax components are available.
Return the evidence pack a CA reviews before manually filing on the GST portal.

Return device health without ever exposing token digests.

post.description:
Ingest synchronized ledgers and vouchers from the office Tally bridge.

Why idempotency: Network flakes over office internet can cause the bridge daemon
to retry POST requests. Using X-Idempotency-Key and ON CONFLICT (firm_id, tally_guid)
guarantees exact once-only processing without duplicating ledger or voucher rows.

parameters.description: Stable key for the exact sync payload
parameters.name: X-Idempotency-Key

schema.description: Stable key for the exact sync payload
schema.title: X-Idempotency-Key
Response Push Tally Data Api Tally Push Post

post.description: Resume only the caller's persistent workflow run.

get.description: Read only the caller's persistent workflow run.
Process liveness only; no dependencies are consulted.
